bullets.text: Marine Le Pen has announced her candidacy for the French presidency, ending months of speculation. This comes after a court ruled she could run but would have to wear a tag for a year.
A woman suspected of a bomb attack in Monaco has been found dead in Ukraine. Police had been searching for Anastasiia Berezovska after a Ukrainian millionaire was injured in the blast.
Jailers and officials at Russia's 'torture prisons' in Ukraine have been exposed by the BBC. Former prisoners accuse these individuals of abuse in detention centers and are seeking their prosecution.
Telstra's mobile network experienced a major outage across Australia, affecting its customers and those of other providers using its network.
Parents of a child abused by a childcare worker are advocating for urgent reforms to the out-of-school-hours care system to enhance child protection.
The Australian Prime Minister is scheduled to host leaders from Tonga, Samoa, and Papua New Guinea in Brisbane, coinciding with a State of Origin decider.

The US has revoked a sanctions waiver that allowed Iran to sell oil globally, reversing a key element of an interim peace deal following recent attacks in the Strait of Hormuz. This decision caused oil prices to climb almost 6%.

US technology shares declined after Samsung Electronics Co.'s earnings failed to meet investor expectations, raising concerns about the sustained growth of artificial intelligence investments. This led to a selloff in chipmakers.

Options traders are increasingly betting that the broader market is overestimating the extent to which the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates this year, despite current pricing for continued hikes.
